name: flask-babelex

metadata.name: Flask-BabelEx
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Adds i18n/l10n support to Flask applications

documentation:
# Flask-BabelEx

Adds i18n/l10n support to Flask applications with the help of the
[Babel] library.

This is fork of official Flask-Babel extension with following features:

1. It is possible to use multiple language catalogs in one Flask application;
2. Localization domains: your extension can package localization file(s) and use them
   if necessary;
3. Does not reload localizations for each request.

## Links

- [documentation](http://packages.python.org/Flask-BabelEx)
- [development version](http://github.com/mrjoes/flask-babelex/zipball/master#egg=Flask-BabelEx-dev)
- [original Flask-Babel extension](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/Flask-Babel).

[babel]: http://babel.edgewall.org/

modules.name: flask_babelex
documentation:
flask_babelex
~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Implements i18n/l10n support for Flask applications based on Babel.

:copyright: (c) 2013 by Serge S. Koval, Armin Ronacher and contributors.
:license: BSD, see LICENSE for more details.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: flask_babelex.get_locale
documentation:
Returns the locale that should be used for this request as
`babel.Locale` object.  This returns `None` if used outside of
a request. If flask-babel was not attached to the Flask application,
will return 'en' locale.

functions.name: flask_babelex.get_timezone
documentation:
Returns the timezone that should be used for this request as
`pytz.timezone` object.  This returns `None` if used outside of
a request. If flask-babel was not attached to application, will
return UTC timezone object.

functions.name: flask_babelex.refresh
documentation:
Refreshes the cached timezones and locale information. This can
be used to switch a translation between a request and if you want
the changes to take place immediately, not just with the next request:

```
user.timezone = request.form['timezone']
user.locale = request.form['locale']
refresh()
flash(gettext('Language was changed'))
```

Without that refresh, the {func}`~flask.flash` function would probably
return English text and a now German page.

functions.name: flask_babelex._get_format
documentation:
A small helper for the datetime formatting functions.  Looks up
format defaults for different kinds.

functions.name: flask_babelex.to_user_timezone
documentation:
Convert a datetime object to the user's timezone. This automatically
happens on all date formatting unless rebasing is disabled. If you need
to convert a {class}`datetime.datetime` object at any time to the user's
timezone (as returned by {func}`get_timezone` this function can be used).

functions.name: flask_babelex.to_utc
documentation:
Convert a datetime object to UTC and drop tzinfo. This is the
opposite operation to {func}`to_user_timezone`.

functions.name: flask_babelex.format_datetime
documentation:
Return a date formatted according to the given pattern. If no
{class}`~datetime.datetime` object is passed, the current time is
assumed. By default rebasing happens which causes the object to
be converted to the users's timezone (as returned by
{func}`to_user_timezone`). This function formats both date and
time.

The format parameter can either be `'short'`, `'medium'`,
`'long'` or `'full'` (in which cause the language's default for
that setting is used, or the default from the {attr}`Babel.date_formats`
mapping is used) or a format string as documented by Babel.

This function is also available in the template context as filter
named `datetimeformat`.

functions.name: flask_babelex.format_date
documentation:
Return a date formatted according to the given pattern. If no
{class}`~datetime.datetime` or {class}`~datetime.date` object is passed,
the current time is assumed. By default rebasing happens which causes
the object to be converted to the users's timezone (as returned by
{func}`to_user_timezone`). This function only formats the date part
of a {class}`~datetime.datetime` object.

The format parameter can either be `'short'`, `'medium'`,
`'long'` or `'full'` (in which cause the language's default for
that setting is used, or the default from the {attr}`Babel.date_formats`
mapping is used) or a format string as documented by Babel.

This function is also available in the template context as filter
named `dateformat`.

functions.name: flask_babelex.format_time
documentation:
Return a time formatted according to the given pattern. If no
{class}`~datetime.datetime` object is passed, the current time is
assumed. By default rebasing happens which causes the object to
be converted to the users's timezone (as returned by
{func}`to_user_timezone`). This function formats both date and
time.

The format parameter can either be `'short'`, `'medium'`,
`'long'` or `'full'` (in which cause the language's default for
that setting is used, or the default from the {attr}`Babel.date_formats`
mapping is used) or a format string as documented by Babel.

This function is also available in the template context as filter
named `timeformat`.

functions.name: flask_babelex.format_timedelta
documentation:
Format the elapsed time from the given date to now or the given
timedelta.  This currently requires an unreleased development
version of Babel.

This function is also available in the template context as filter
named `timedeltaformat`.

functions.name: flask_babelex._date_format
documentation: Internal helper that formats the date.

functions.name: flask_babelex.format_number
documentation:
Return the given number formatted for the locale in request

```
param number

the number to format

return

the formatted number

rtype

unicode
```

functions.name: flask_babelex.format_decimal
documentation:
Return the given decimal number formatted for the locale in request

```
param number

the number to format

param format

the format to use

return

the formatted number

rtype

unicode
```

functions.name: flask_babelex.format_currency
documentation:
Return the given number formatted for the locale in request

```
param number

the number to format

param currency

the currency code

param format

the format to use

return

the formatted number

rtype

unicode
```

functions.name: flask_babelex.format_percent
documentation:
Return formatted percent value for the locale in request

```
param number

the number to format

param format

the format to use

return

the formatted percent number

rtype

unicode
```

functions.name: flask_babelex.format_scientific
documentation:
Return value formatted in scientific notation for the locale in request

```
param number

the number to format

param format

the format to use

return

the formatted percent number

rtype

unicode
```

functions.name: flask_babelex.get_domain
documentation:
Return the correct translation domain that is used for this request.
This will return the default domain (e.g. "messages" in <approot>/translations")
if none is set for this request.

functions.name: flask_babelex.gettext
documentation: Create shortcuts for the default Flask domain

classes.name: flask_babelex.Babel

methods.name: flask_babelex.Babel.init_app
documentation:
Set up this instance for use with *app*, if no app was passed to
the constructor.

methods.name: flask_babelex.Babel.localeselector
documentation:
Registers a callback function for locale selection. The default
behaves as if a function was registered that returns `None` all the
time. If `None` is returned, the locale falls back to the one from
the configuration.

This has to return the locale as string (eg: `'de_AT'`, ''`en_US`'')

methods.name: flask_babelex.Babel.timezoneselector
documentation:
Registers a callback function for timezone selection. The default
behaves as if a function was registered that returns `None` all the
time. If `None` is returned, the timezone falls back to the one from
the configuration.

This has to return the timezone as string (eg: `'Europe/Vienna'`)

methods.name: flask_babelex.Babel.list_translations
documentation:
Returns a list of all the locales translations exist for. The
list returned will be filled with actual locale objects and not just
strings.

:::{versionadded} 0.6
:::

methods.name: flask_babelex.Babel.default_locale
documentation:
The default locale from the configuration as instance of a
`babel.Locale` object.

methods.name: flask_babelex.Babel.default_timezone
documentation:
The default timezone from the configuration as instance of a
`pytz.timezone` object.

methods.name: flask_babelex.Babel.load_locale
documentation:
Load locale by name and cache it. Returns instance of a `babel.Locale`
object.

Central controller class that can be used to configure how
Flask-Babel behaves. Each application that wants to use Flask-Babel
has to create, or run {meth}`init_app` on, an instance of this class
after the configuration was initialized.

classes.name: flask_babelex.Domain

methods.name: flask_babelex.Domain.as_default
documentation: Set this domain as default for the current request

methods.name: flask_babelex.Domain.get_translations_cache
documentation: Returns dictionary-like object for translation caching

methods.name: flask_babelex.Domain.get_translations_path
documentation:
Returns translations directory path. Override if you want
to implement custom behavior.

methods.name: flask_babelex.Domain.get_translations
documentation:
Returns the correct gettext translations that should be used for
this request.  This will never fail and return a dummy translation
object if used outside of the request or if a translation cannot be
found.

methods.name: flask_babelex.Domain.gettext
documentation:
Translates a string with the current locale and passes in the
given keyword arguments as mapping to a string formatting string.

```
gettext(u'Hello World!')
gettext(u'Hello %(name)s!', name='World')
```

methods.name: flask_babelex.Domain.ngettext
documentation:
Translates a string with the current locale and passes in the
given keyword arguments as mapping to a string formatting string.
The `num` parameter is used to dispatch between singular and various
plural forms of the message. It is available in the format string
as `%(num)d` or `%(num)s`. The source language should be
English or a similar language which only has one plural form.

```
ngettext(u'%(num)d Apple', u'%(num)d Apples', num=len(apples))
```

methods.name: flask_babelex.Domain.pgettext
documentation:
Like {func}`gettext` but with a context.

:::{versionadded} 0.7
:::

methods.name: flask_babelex.Domain.npgettext
documentation:
Like {func}`ngettext` but with a context.

:::{versionadded} 0.7
:::

methods.name: flask_babelex.Domain.lazy_gettext
flask_babelex.Domain.lazy_pgettext
documentation:
Like {func}`pgettext` but the string returned is lazy which means
it will be translated when it is used as an actual string.

:::{versionadded} 0.7
:::

Localization domain. By default will use look for tranlations in Flask application directory
and "messages" domain - all message catalogs should be called `messages.mo`.
